title: Paneion sandbergii
intro: Paneion sandbergii, also known as Western sweetroot, is a perennial plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The health benefits of Western sweetroot include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various conditions such as digestive issues and infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene glycosides, sesquiterpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Western sweetroot is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support digestive health and immune function.
